Plaintiff Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production; And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-18-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Constantin Roboostoff, Esq. (415-732-0282) of Roboostoff & Kalkin for Plaintiff PAUL PERDUE, JOY PERDUE.
Joanna Kozubal, Esq. (415-864-6962) for Defendant Louie Kinry.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues sanctions. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT PAUL PERDUE, JOY PERDUE'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production And For Monetary Sanctions.

Plaintiffs Paul and Joy Perdue's motion to compel further responses to request for production and for monetary sanctions is granted. The court adopts in full the recommendations and reasoning of the report by Judge Pro Tem James Fleming. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order signed in open court. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Gordon Aiavao, CSR No: 11216, email: gaiavao@sftc.org. (D302)

Plaintiff Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production; And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-28-2017 Continued To Oct-18-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Constantin Roboostoff, Esq. of Roboostoff & Kalkin, Tel. 415.732.0282, for plaintiffs and moving party;
Joanna Kozubal, Esq., Tel. 415.864.6962, for defendant and opposing party.

Defendant's counsel did not stipulate to the judge pro tem.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court vacated its tentative ruling and continued PLAINTIFFS PAUL PERDUE and JOY PERDUE's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production; And For Monetary Sanctions to October 18, 2017 at 9:30 am in Department 302. The Judge Pro Tem will prepare a Report and Recommendation. Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
